Feature_Name: Inter gNB DU LTM Handover UE Context Setup
Feature_Description: This procedure involves the gNB-CU preparing and sending an F1AP "UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST" message to a candidate gNB-DU. The candidate gNB-DU then responds with an F1AP "UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE" message. This message exchange is specifically handled on the gNB-CU for Inter gNB DU LTM Handover.
LTM (Long Term Mobility) configuration procedures are performed between the gNB-CU and candidate gNB-DUs, and between the gNB-CU and the source gNB-DU as part of LTM with gNB-CU-UP change.

An NG-RAN node UE context is a block of information in an NG-RAN node associated with one UE. It contains the necessary information required to maintain NG-RAN services towards the active UE. This context is established in the target NG-RAN node after completion of handover resource allocation during handover preparation, including at least UE state information, security information, UE capability information, and UE-associated logical NG-connection identities.
Feature_Objectives: To establish or modify the UE context in a candidate gNB-DU during an Inter gNB DU LTM Handover procedure.
To enable the gNB-DU to perform delta or full configuration based on the received UE context information.
To facilitate the continuation of NG-RAN services for the UE during LTM Handover.
Procedure_Implementation_Steps: The gNB-CU initiates LTM configuration procedures with candidate gNB-DUs and the source gNB-DU (as specified in section 8.2.1.5 for LTM configuration procedures).
The gNB-CU prepares and sends the F1AP "UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST" message to the candidate gNB-DU to create a UE context. The gNB-CU allocates a gNB-CU UE F1AP ID for the UE.

The "UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST" message may contain source cell group configuration (e.g., CG-ConfigInfo) to allow the gNB-DU to perform delta configuration. It may also include SRB ID(s) and DRB ID(s) to be setup, CellGroupConfig, and stored F1 UL TEIDs.
The candidate gNB-DU responds to the gNB-CU with the F1AP "UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE" message.

In case the gNB-DU receives the "UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST" message without the source cell group configuration, or if it decides to perform full configuration after receiving the source cell group configuration, it shall perform full configuration and indicate that it has applied full configuration in the "UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE" message.
The "UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE" message contains RLC/MAC/PHY configuration of SRB and DRBs provided by the gNB-DU, and includes the F1 DL TEIDs allocated for the DRBs.

Code_Instructions.description: Defines the structure for the F1AP UE Context Setup Request message, including all Information Elements (IEs) required for establishing or modifying a UE context at the gNB-DU during an Inter gNB DU LTM Handover.
Defines the structure for the F1AP UE Context Setup Response message, including IEs indicating the success or failure of the UE context setup and any related information returned by the gNB-DU.

A helper function responsible for populating the `f1ap_ue_context_setup_req_s` data structure with the necessary information (e.g., UE capabilities, security context, PDU session information) before the F1AP UE Context Setup Request message is sent.

This function likely handles the encoding of the F1AP UE Context Setup Request message into a transmittable format or performs initial processing/validation of the message content within the RRC layer for F1AP interaction.

A utility function to retrieve UE-specific data from the gNB-CU's internal context management, which is essential for populating the F1AP UE Context Setup Request message with relevant UE information (e.g., F1AP UE IDs, RRC context).

Piggy_Backed_IES_Info: Not applicable for the scoped messages in this procedure.
